```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Foster and homeless youth K-12 students struggle to receive an education compared to their peers.",
    "Almost half of all foster youth struggle with chronic absenteeism due to frequent moves from school to school.",
    "Frequent moves make it hard for students to engage in their education and maintain the appropriate number of credits to graduate.",
    "High school graduation is critical to long-term success for foster and homeless youth.",
    "Moving schools forces students to learn a new education system, new teachers, new expectations, and a new curriculum.",
    "Access to educational programs during school breaks can combat learning loss for foster and homeless youth.",
    "Periods of school breaks are times when youth can get disconnected from academic and social supports.",
    "Educational disparities exist for foster youth and students experiencing homelessness, two historically underserved populations.",
    "Disruptions to living situations significantly impact the academic achievement and well-being of foster youth and homeless students.",
    "AB 373 aims to ensure all school districts prioritize additional educational resources and support for underserved youth.",
    "Students in foster care and those experiencing homelessness encounter challenges in school due to factors outside of their control.",
    "Educational gaps exist due to school mobility caused by placement changes or housing instability for foster and homeless youth."
  ]
}
```
